   312→    def _copy_and_notify(self, text: str) -> None:
   313→        """Copy text to clipboard and show notification.
   314→
   315→        Args:
   316→            text: Text to copy to clipboard
   317→        """
   318→        if self._clipboard is not None:
   319→            self._clipboard.copy(text)
   320→        # Show brief notification via app's notify method
   321→        self.notify(f"Copied: {text}", timeout=2)
   322→
   323→    def action_copy_checkout(self) -> None:
   324→        """Copy local checkout command to clipboard."""
   325→        if self._row.worktree_branch is None:
   326→            self.notify(
   327→                "No branch associated with this plan is checked out in a local worktree",
   328→                severity="warning",
   329→            )
   330→            return
   331→        cmd = f"erk br co {self._row.worktree_branch}"
   332→        self._copy_and_notify(cmd)
   333→
   334→    def action_copy_pr_checkout(self) -> None:
   335→        """Copy PR checkout command to clipboard."""
   336→        if self._row.pr_number is not None:
   337→            cmd = f"erk pr co {self._row.pr_number}"
   338→            self._copy_and_notify(cmd)
   339→
   340→    def action_copy_prepare(self) -> None:
   341→        """Copy basic prepare command to clipboard."""
   342→        cmd = f"erk br co --for-plan {self._row.plan_id}"
   343→        self._copy_and_notify(cmd)
   344→
   345→    def action_copy_prepare_activate(self) -> None:
   346→        """Copy one-liner to prepare worktree and start implementation."""
   347→        cmd = (
   348→            f'source "$(erk br co --for-plan {self._row.plan_id} --script)"'
   349→            " && erk implement --dangerous"
   350→        )
   351→        self._copy_and_notify(cmd)
   352→
   353→    def action_copy_implement_local(self) -> None:
   354→        """Copy one-liner to checkout PR and start local implementation."""
   355→        if self._row.pr_number is not None:
   356→            cmd = (
   357→                f'source "$(erk pr checkout {self._row.pr_number} --script)"'
   358→                " && erk implement --dangerous"
   359→            )
   360→            self._copy_and_notify(cmd)
   361→
   362→    def action_copy_submit(self) -> None:
   363→        """Copy submit command to clipboard."""
   364→        cmd = f"erk pr dispatch {self._row.plan_id}"
   365→        self._copy_and_notify(cmd)
   366→
   367→    def action_fix_conflicts_remote(self) -> None:
   368→        """Launch remote conflict resolution workflow."""
   369→
   370→        if self._row.pr_number is None or self._repo_root is None:
   371→            return
   372→        self.run_streaming_command(
   373→            ["erk", "launch", "pr-fix-conflicts", "--pr", str(self._row.pr_number)],
   374→            cwd=self._repo_root,
   375→            title=f"Fix Conflicts Remote PR #{self._row.pr_number}",
   376→        )
   377→
   378→    def action_copy_output_logs(self) -> None:
   379→        """Copy command output logs to clipboard."""
   380→        if self._output_panel is None:
   381→            return
   382→        if not self._output_panel.is_completed:
   383→            return
   384→        self._copy_and_notify(self._output_panel.get_output_text())
   385→
   386→    async def action_dismiss(self, result: object = None) -> None:
   387→        """Dismiss the modal, blocking while command is running.
   388→
   389→        Args:
   390→            result: Optional result to pass to dismiss (unused, for API compat)
   391→        """
   392→        # Block while command is running
   393→        if self._command_running:
   394→            return
   395→
   396→        # If panel exists and completed, refresh data if successful
   397→        if self._output_panel is not None:
   398→            if self._output_panel.is_completed:
   399→                if self._executor and self._output_panel.succeeded:
   400→                    self._executor.refresh_data()
   401→                await self._flush_next_callbacks()
   402→                self.dismiss(result)
   403→            return
   404→
   405→        # Normal dismiss
   406→        await self._flush_next_callbacks()
   407→        self.dismiss(result)
   408→
   409→    def run_streaming_command(
   410→        self,
   411→        command: list[str],
   412→        cwd: Path,
   413→        title: str,
   414→        *,
   415→        timeout: float = 30.0,
   416→        on_success: Callable[[], None] | None = None,
   417→    ) -> None:
   418→        """Run command with live output in bottom panel.
   419→
   420→        Args:
   421→            command: Command to run as list of arguments
   422→            cwd: Working directory for the command
   423→            title: Title to display in the output panel
   424→            timeout: Timeout in seconds (default 30). Set to 0 to disable.
   425→            on_success: Optional callback to invoke on successful completion.
   426→        """
   427→        # Create and mount output panel
   428→        self._output_panel = CommandOutputPanel(title)
   429→        dialog = self.query_one("#detail-dialog")
   430→        dialog.mount(self._output_panel)
   431→        self._command_running = True
   432→        self._on_success_callback = on_success
   433→
   434→        # Set timeout timer if enabled
   435→        if timeout > 0:
   436→            self._stream_timeout_seconds = timeout
   437→            self._stream_timeout_timer = self.set_timer(timeout, self._handle_stream_timeout)
   438→
   439→        # Run subprocess in worker thread
   440→        self._stream_subprocess(command, cwd)
   441→
   442→    def run_close_plan_in_process(
   443→        self,
   444→        plan_id: int,
   445→        plan_url: str,
   446→    ) -> None:
   447→        """Run close plan in-process using HTTP client directly.
   448→
   449→        This is much faster than spawning a subprocess because it uses
   450→        the HTTP client that was initialized at TUI startup.
   451→
   452→        Args:
   453→            plan_id: The plan identifier to close
   454→            plan_url: The plan URL for PR linkage lookup
   455→        """
   456→        # Create and mount output panel
   457→        title = f"Closing Plan #{plan_id}"
   458→        self._output_panel = CommandOutputPanel(title)
   459→        dialog = self.query_one("#detail-dialog")
   460→        dialog.mount(self._output_panel)
   461→        self._command_running = True
   462→
   463→        # Run in-process worker (no timeout needed - HTTP has its own timeout)
   464→        self._run_close_plan_worker(plan_id, plan_url)
   465→
   466→    @work(thread=True)
   467→    def _run_close_plan_worker(
   468→        self,
   469→        plan_id: int,
   470→        plan_url: str,
   471→    ) -> None:
   472→        """Worker: close plan using executor's close_plan_fn.
   473→
   474→        Args:
   475→            plan_id: The plan identifier to close
   476→            plan_url: The plan URL for PR linkage lookup
   477→        """
   478→        panel = self._output_panel
   479→        if panel is None or self._executor is None:
   480→            self._command_running = False
   481→            return
   482→
   483→        success = True
   484→        # Error boundary: catch all exceptions from HTTP operations to display
   485→        # them in the output panel rather than crashing the TUI.
   486→        try:
   487→            self.app.call_from_thread(panel.append_line, "Closing linked PRs...")
   488→            closed_prs = self._executor.close_plan(plan_id, plan_url)
   489→
   490→            if closed_prs:
   491→                pr_list = ", ".join(f"#{pr}" for pr in closed_prs)
   492→                self.app.call_from_thread(panel.append_line, f"Closed PRs: {pr_list}")
   493→
   494→            self.app.call_from_thread(panel.append_line, f"Closed plan #{plan_id}")
   495→        except Exception as e:
   496→            self.app.call_from_thread(panel.append_line, f"Error: {e}")
   497→            success = False
   498→
   499→        self.app.call_from_thread(panel.set_completed, success)
   500→        self._command_running = False
   501→
   502→    def _handle_stream_timeout(self) -> None:
   503→        """Handle timeout of streaming command.
   504→
   505→        Called by Textual timer when the subprocess exceeds the timeout.
   506→        Kills the process and marks the command as failed.
   507→        """
   508→        # Clear timer reference since it has fired
   509→        self._stream_timeout_timer = None
   510→
   511→        # Check if process is still running
   512→        process = self._running_process
   513→        if process is None:
   514→            return
   515→
   516→        # Kill the process if still running (LBYL pattern)
   517→        if process.poll() is None:
   518→            process.kill()
   519→
   520→        # Append timeout message to output panel
   521→        panel = self._output_panel
   522→        if panel is not None:
   523→            panel.append_line("")
   524→            # Format timeout as minutes if >= 60 seconds for readability
   525→            timeout_secs = self._stream_timeout_seconds
   526→            if timeout_secs >= 60:
   527→                minutes = int(timeout_secs // 60)
   528→                timeout_str = f"{minutes} minute{'s' if minutes != 1 else ''}"
   529→            else:
   530→                timeout_str = f"{int(timeout_secs)} seconds"
   531→            panel.append_line(f"⏱️  Command timed out after {timeout_str}")
   532→            panel.set_completed(success=False)
   533→
   534→        # Mark command as no longer running
   535→        self._command_running = False
   536→
   537→    @work(thread=True)
   538→    def _stream_subprocess(self, command: list[str], cwd: Path) -> None:
   539→        """Worker: stream subprocess output to panel.
   540→
   541→        Args:
   542→            command: Command to run
   543→            cwd: Working directory
   544→        """
   545→        # Capture panel reference at start (won't be None since run_streaming_command sets it)
   546→        panel = self._output_panel
   547→        if panel is None:
   548→            self._command_running = False
   549→            return
   550→
   551→        process = subprocess.Popen(
   552→            command,
   553→            cwd=cwd,
   554→            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
   555→            stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
   556→            stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   557→            text=True,
   558→            bufsize=1,
   559→        )
   560→
   561→        # Store process reference for timeout handler
   562→        self._running_process = process
   563→
   564→        if process.stdout is not None:
   565→            for line in process.stdout:
   566→                self.app.call_from_thread(
   567→                    panel.append_line,
   568→                    line.rstrip(),
   569→                )
   570→
   571→        return_code = process.wait()
   572→
   573→        # Clear process reference
   574→        self._running_process = None
   575→
   576→        # Cancel timeout timer if still active
   577→        timer = self._stream_timeout_timer
   578→        if timer is not None:
   579→            timer.stop()
   580→            self._stream_timeout_timer = None
   581→
   582→        # Only mark complete if timeout handler hasn't already done so
   583→        if self._command_running:
   584→            success = return_code == 0
   585→            self.app.call_from_thread(panel.set_completed, success)
   586→            self._command_running = False
   587→            # Invoke success callback if provided and command succeeded
   588→            if success:
   589→                callback = self._on_success_callback
   590→                if callback is not None:
   591→                    self.app.call_from_thread(callback)
   592→
   593→    def execute_command(self, command_id: str) -> None:
   594→        """Execute a command from the palette.
   595→
   596→        Args:
   597→            command_id: The ID of the command to execute
   598→        """
   599→        # Import here to avoid circular import at module level
   600→        from erk.tui.app import ErkDashApp
   601→
   602→        if self._executor is None:
   603→            return
   604→
   605→        row = self._row
   606→        executor = self._executor
   607→
   608→        if command_id == "open_browser":
   609→            url = row.pr_url or row.plan_url
   610→            if url:
   611→                executor.open_url(url)
   612→                executor.notify(f"Opened {url}", severity=None)
   613→
   614→        elif command_id == "open_issue":
   615→            if row.plan_url:
   616→                executor.open_url(row.plan_url)
   617→                executor.notify(f"Opened plan #{row.plan_id}", severity=None)
   618→
   619→        elif command_id == "open_pr":
   620→            if row.pr_url:
   621→                executor.open_url(row.pr_url)
   622→                executor.notify(f"Opened PR #{row.pr_number}", severity=None)
   623→
   624→        elif command_id == "open_run":
   625→            if row.run_url:
   626→                executor.open_url(row.run_url)
   627→                executor.notify(f"Opened run {row.run_id_display}", severity=None)
   628→
   629→        elif command_id == "copy_checkout":
   630→            if row.worktree_branch is None:
   631→                executor.notify(
   632→                    "No branch associated with this plan is checked out in a local worktree",
   633→                    severity="warning",
   634→                )
   635→                return
   636→            cmd = f"erk br co {row.worktree_branch}"
   637→            executor.copy_to_clipboard(cmd)
   638→            executor.notify(f"Copied: {cmd}", severity=None)
   639→
   640→        elif command_id == "copy_pr_checkout":
   641→            cmd = f"erk pr co {row.pr_number}"
   642→            executor.copy_to_clipboard(cmd)
   643→            executor.notify(f"Copied: {cmd}", severity=None)
   644→
   645→        elif command_id == "copy_prepare":
   646→            cmd = f"erk br co --for-plan {row.plan_id}"
   647→            executor.copy_to_clipboard(cmd)
   648→            executor.notify(f"Copied: {cmd}", severity=None)
   649→
   650→        elif command_id == "copy_prepare_activate":
   651→            cmd = (
   652→                f'source "$(erk br co --for-plan {row.plan_id} --script)"'
   653→                " && erk implement --dangerous"
   654→            )
   655→            executor.copy_to_clipboard(cmd)
   656→            executor.notify(f"Copied: {cmd}", severity=None)
   657→
   658→        elif command_id == "copy_implement_local":
   659→            if row.pr_number is not None:
   660→                cmd = (
   661→                    f'source "$(erk pr checkout {row.pr_number} --script)"'
   662→                    " && erk implement --dangerous"
   663→                )
   664→                executor.copy_to_clipboard(cmd)
   665→                executor.notify(f"Copied: {cmd}", severity=None)
   666→
   667→        elif command_id == "copy_submit":
   668→            cmd = f"erk pr dispatch {row.plan_id}"
   669→            executor.copy_to_clipboard(cmd)
   670→            executor.notify(f"Copied: {cmd}", severity=None)
   671→
   672→        elif command_id == "copy_replan":
   673→            cmd = f"erk pr replan {row.plan_id}"
   674→            executor.copy_to_clipboard(cmd)
   675→            executor.notify(f"Copied: {cmd}", severity=None)
   676→
   677→        elif command_id == "copy_land":
   678→            if row.pr_number is not None:
   679→                cmd = f"erk land {row.pr_number}"
   680→                executor.copy_to_clipboard(cmd)
   681→                executor.notify(f"Copied: {cmd}", severity=None)
   682→
   683→        elif command_id == "copy_close_plan":
   684→            cmd = f"erk pr close {row.plan_id}"
   685→            executor.copy_to_clipboard(cmd)
   686→            executor.notify(f"Copied: {cmd}", severity=None)
   687→
   688→        elif command_id == "copy_fix_conflicts_remote":
   689→            if row.pr_number is not None:
   690→                cmd = f"erk launch pr-fix-conflicts --pr {row.pr_number}"
   691→                executor.copy_to_clipboard(cmd)
   692→                executor.notify(f"Copied: {cmd}", severity=None)
   693→
   694→        elif command_id == "copy_address_remote":
   695→            if row.pr_number is not None:
   696→                cmd = f"erk launch pr-address --pr {row.pr_number}"
   697→                executor.copy_to_clipboard(cmd)
   698→                executor.notify(f"Copied: {cmd}", severity=None)
   699→
   700→        elif command_id == "fix_conflicts_remote":
   701→            if row.pr_number is not None and self._repo_root is not None:
   702→                self.run_streaming_command(
   703→                    ["erk", "launch", "pr-fix-conflicts", "--pr", str(row.pr_number)],
   704→                    cwd=self._repo_root,
   705→                    title=f"Fix Conflicts Remote PR #{row.pr_number}",
   706→                )
   707→
   708→        elif command_id == "address_remote":
   709→            if row.pr_number is not None and self._repo_root is not None:
   710→                self.run_streaming_command(
   711→                    ["erk", "launch", "pr-address", "--pr", str(row.pr_number)],
   712→                    cwd=self._repo_root,
   713→                    title=f"Address Remote PR #{row.pr_number}",
   714→                )
   715→
   716→        elif command_id == "close_plan":
   717→            if row.plan_url:
   718→                # Dismiss detail screen first, then run async close on main app
   719→                self.dismiss()
   720→                # Access parent app and trigger async close with toast
   721→                if isinstance(self.app, ErkDashApp):
   722→                    self.app.notify(f"Closing plan #{row.plan_id}...")
   723→                    self.app._close_plan_async(row.plan_id, row.plan_url)
   724→
   725→        elif command_id == "submit_to_queue":
   726→            if row.plan_url:
   727→                self.dismiss()
   728→                if isinstance(self.app, ErkDashApp):
   729→                    self.app.notify(f"Dispatching plan #{row.plan_id} to queue...")
   730→                    self.app._dispatch_to_queue_async(row.plan_id)
   731→
   732→        elif command_id == "land_pr":
   733→            if row.pr_number and row.pr_head_branch:
   734→                self.dismiss()
   735→                if isinstance(self.app, ErkDashApp):
   736→                    self.app.notify(f"Landing PR #{row.pr_number}...")
   737→                    self.app._land_pr_async(row.pr_number, row.pr_head_branch, row.objective_issue)
   738→
   739→    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
   740→        """Create detail dialog content as an Action Hub."""
   741→        with Vertical(id="detail-dialog"):
   742→            # Header: Plan number + title
   743→            with Vertical(id="detail-header"):
   744→                plan_text = f"Plan #{self._row.plan_id}"
   745→                yield Label(plan_text, id="detail-plan-link")
   746→                yield Label(self._row.full_title, id="detail-title", markup=False)
   747→
   748→            # Divider
   749→            yield Label("", id="detail-divider")
   750→
   751→            # PLAN/PR INFO SECTION
   752→            # Plan Info - clickable plan number
   753→            with Container(classes="info-row"):
   754→                yield Label("Plan", classes="info-label")
   755→                if self._row.plan_url:
   756→                    yield ClickableLink(
   757→                        f"#{self._row.plan_id}", self._row.plan_url, classes="info-value"
   758→                    )
   759→                else:
   760→                    yield Label(f"#{self._row.plan_id}", classes="info-value", markup=False)
   761→
   762→            # PR Info (if exists) - clickable PR number with state badge inline
   763→            if self._row.pr_number:
   764→                with Container(classes="info-row"):
   765→                    yield Label("PR", classes="info-label")
   766→                    if self._row.pr_url:
   767→                        yield ClickableLink(
   768→                            f"#{self._row.pr_number}", self._row.pr_url, classes="info-value"
   769→                        )
   770→                    else:
   771→                        yield Label(f"#{self._row.pr_number}", classes="info-value", markup=False)
   772→                    # PR state badge inline
   773→                    pr_text, pr_class = self._get_pr_state_badge()
   774→                    yield Label(pr_text, classes=f"status-badge {pr_class}")
   775→
   776→                # PR title if different from issue title
   777→                if self._row.pr_title and self._row.pr_title != self._row.full_title:
   778→                    with Container(classes="info-row"):
   779→                        yield Label("PR Title", classes="info-label")
   780→                        yield Label(self._row.pr_title, classes="info-value", markup=False)
   781→
   782→                # Checks status
   783→                if self._row.checks_display and self._row.checks_display != "-":
   784→                    with Container(classes="info-row"):
   785→                        yield Label("Checks", classes="info-label")
   786→                        yield Label(self._row.checks_display, classes="info-value", markup=False)
   787→
   788→            # Learn status - always show for visibility into learn workflow
   789→            with Container(classes="info-row"):
   790→                yield Label("Learn", classes="info-label")
   791→                # Make clickable if there's a plan issue, PR, or workflow run
   792→                if self._row.learn_plan_pr is not None and self._row.plan_url:
   793→                    base_url = self._row.plan_url.rsplit("/issues/", 1)[0]
   794→                    pr_url = f"{base_url}/pull/{self._row.learn_plan_pr}"
   795→                    yield ClickableLink(self._row.learn_display, pr_url, classes="info-value")
   796→                elif self._row.learn_plan_issue is not None and self._row.plan_url:
   797→                    base_url = self._row.plan_url.rsplit("/issues/", 1)[0]
   798→                    issue_url = f"{base_url}/issues/{self._row.learn_plan_issue}"
   799→                    yield ClickableLink(self._row.learn_display, issue_url, classes="info-value")
   800→                elif self._row.learn_run_url is not None:
   801→                    yield ClickableLink(
   802→                        self._row.learn_display, self._row.learn_run_url, classes="info-value"
   803→                    )
   804→                else:
   805→                    yield Label(self._row.learn_display, classes="info-value", markup=False)
   806→
   807→            # Objective info (if linked)
   808→            if self._row.objective_issue is not None:
   809→                with Container(classes="info-row"):
   810→                    yield Label("Objective", classes="info-label")
   811→                    display_text = (
   812→                        f"{self._row.objective_display} ({self._row.objective_progress_display})"
   813→                        if self._row.objective_progress_display != "-"
   814→                        else self._row.objective_display
   815→                    )
   816→                    if self._row.plan_url:
   817→                        objective_url = (
   818→                            self._row.plan_url.rsplit("/issues/", 1)[0]
   819→                            + f"/issues/{self._row.objective_issue}"
   820→                        )
   821→                        yield ClickableLink(display_text, objective_url, classes="info-value")
   822→                    else:
   823→                        yield Label(display_text, classes="info-value", markup=False)
   824→
   825→            # REMOTE RUN INFO SECTION (separate from worktree/local info)
   826→            if self._row.run_id:
   827→                with Container(classes="info-row"):
   828→                    yield Label("Run", classes="info-label")
   829→                    if self._row.run_url:
   830→                        yield ClickableLink(
   831→                            self._row.run_id, self._row.run_url, classes="info-value"
   832→                        )
   833→                    else:
   834→                        yield Label(self._row.run_id, classes="info-value", markup=False)
   835→                    # Run status badge inline
   836→                    run_text, run_class = self._get_run_badge()
   837→                    yield Label(run_text, classes=f"status-badge {run_class}")
   838→
   839→                if self._row.remote_impl_display and self._row.remote_impl_display != "-":
   840→                    with Container(classes="info-row"):
   841→                        yield Label("Last remote impl", classes="info-label")
   842→                        yield Label(
   843→                            self._row.remote_impl_display, classes="info-value", markup=False
   844→                        )
   845→
   846→            # COMMANDS SECTION (copy to clipboard)
   847→            # All items below use uniform orange labels that copy when clicked
   848→            yield Label("COMMANDS (copy)", classes="section-header")
   849→
   850→            # PR checkout command (if PR exists)
   851→            if self._row.pr_number is not None:
   852→                pr_checkout_cmd = f"erk pr co {self._row.pr_number}"
   853→                with Container(classes="command-row"):
   854→                    yield CopyableLabel(pr_checkout_cmd, pr_checkout_cmd)
   855→
   856→            # Prepare commands
   857→            prepare_cmd = f"erk br co --for-plan {self._row.plan_id}"
   858→            with Container(classes="command-row"):
   859→                yield Label("[1]", classes="command-key")
   860→                yield CopyableLabel(prepare_cmd, prepare_cmd)
   861→
   862→            # Submit command
   863→            dispatch_cmd = f"erk pr dispatch {self._row.plan_id}"
   864→            with Container(classes="command-row"):
   865→                yield Label("[3]", classes="command-key")
   866→                yield CopyableLabel(dispatch_cmd, dispatch_cmd)
   867→
   868→            # Log entries (if any) - clickable timestamps
   869→            if self._row.log_entries:
   870→                with Vertical(classes="log-section"):
   871→                    yield Label("Recent activity", classes="log-header")
   872→                    for event_name, timestamp, comment_url in self._row.log_entries[:5]:
   873→                        log_text = f"{timestamp}  {event_name}"
   874→                        if comment_url:
   875→                            yield ClickableLink(log_text, comment_url, classes="log-entry")
   876→                        else:
   877→                            yield Label(log_text, classes="log-entry", markup=False)
   878→
   879→            yield Label("Ctrl+P: commands  Esc: close", id="detail-footer")
   880→

"""Command registry for command palette.

This module defines all available commands and their availability predicates.
Commands are organized by category: Actions, Opens, Copies.
Plan commands appear only in Plans/Learn views; objective commands appear only in Objectives view.
"""

from erk.tui.commands.types import CommandCategory, CommandContext, CommandDefinition
from erk.tui.views.types import ViewMode

CATEGORY_EMOJI: dict[CommandCategory, str] = {
    CommandCategory.ACTION: "⚡",
    CommandCategory.OPEN: "🔗",
    CommandCategory.COPY: "📋",
}


# === View Mode Predicates ===


def _is_plan_view(ctx: CommandContext) -> bool:
    """True when not in Objectives view (i.e., Plans or Learn)."""
    return ctx.view_mode != ViewMode.OBJECTIVES


def _is_objectives_view(ctx: CommandContext) -> bool:
    """True when in Objectives view."""
    return ctx.view_mode == ViewMode.OBJECTIVES


# === Display Name Generators (Plan Commands) ===


def _display_close_plan(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for close_plan command."""
    return f"erk pr close {ctx.row.plan_id}"


def _display_submit_to_queue(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for submit_to_queue command."""
    return f"erk pr dispatch {ctx.row.plan_id}"


def _display_land_pr(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for land_pr command."""
    return f"erk land {ctx.row.pr_number}"


def _display_fix_conflicts_remote(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for fix_conflicts_remote command."""
    return f"erk launch pr-fix-conflicts --pr {ctx.row.pr_number}"


def _display_address_remote(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for address_remote command."""
    return f"erk launch pr-address --pr {ctx.row.pr_number}"


def _display_open_issue(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for open_issue command."""
    if ctx.row.plan_url:
        return ctx.row.plan_url
    return "Issue"


def _display_open_pr(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for open_pr command."""
    if ctx.row.pr_url:
        return ctx.row.pr_url
    return "PR"


def _display_open_run(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for open_run command."""
    if ctx.row.run_url:
        return ctx.row.run_url
    return "Workflow Run"


def _display_copy_checkout(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_checkout command."""
    if ctx.row.worktree_branch:
        return f"erk br co {ctx.row.worktree_branch}"
    if ctx.row.pr_number:
        return f"erk pr co {ctx.row.pr_number}"
    return "erk br co <branch>"


def _display_copy_pr_checkout(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_pr_checkout command."""
    if ctx.row.pr_number:
        pr = ctx.row.pr_number
        return f'source "$(erk pr checkout {pr} --script --sync)"'
    return "checkout"


def _display_copy_implement_local(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_implement_local command."""
    return f'source "$(erk pr checkout {ctx.row.pr_number} --script)" && erk implement --dangerous'


def _display_copy_submit(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_submit command."""
    return f"erk pr dispatch {ctx.row.plan_id}"


def _display_copy_replan(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_replan command."""
    return f"erk pr replan {ctx.row.plan_id}"


def _display_copy_land(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_land command."""
    return f"erk land {ctx.row.pr_number}"


def _display_copy_close_plan(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_close_plan command."""
    return f"erk pr close {ctx.row.plan_id}"


def _display_copy_fix_conflicts_remote(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_fix_conflicts_remote command."""
    return f"erk launch pr-fix-conflicts --pr {ctx.row.pr_number}"


def _display_copy_address_remote(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_address_remote command."""
    return f"erk launch pr-address --pr {ctx.row.pr_number}"


# === Display Name Generators (Objective Commands) ===


def _display_one_shot_plan(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for one_shot_plan command."""
    return f"erk objective plan {ctx.row.plan_id} --one-shot"


def _display_check_objective(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for check_objective command."""
    return f"erk objective check {ctx.row.plan_id}"


def _display_close_objective(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for close_objective command."""
    return f"erk objective close {ctx.row.plan_id} --force"


def _display_codespace_run_plan(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for codespace_run_plan command."""
    return f"erk codespace run objective plan {ctx.row.plan_id}"


def _display_open_objective(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for open_objective command."""
    if ctx.row.plan_url:
        return ctx.row.plan_url
    return "Objective"


def _display_copy_plan(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_plan command."""
    return f"erk objective plan {ctx.row.plan_id}"


def _display_copy_view(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_view command."""
    return f"erk objective view {ctx.row.plan_id}"


def get_all_commands() -> list[CommandDefinition]:
    """Return all command definitions.

    Commands are ordered by category:
    1. Actions (mutative operations)
    2. Opens (browser navigation)
    3. Copies (clipboard operations)

    Plan commands are filtered out in Objectives view; objective commands
    are filtered out in Plans/Learn views.

    Returns:
        List of all available command definitions
    """
    return [
        # === PLAN ACTIONS ===
        CommandDefinition(
            id="close_plan",
            name="Close Plan",
            description="close",
            category=CommandCategory.ACTION,
            shortcut=None,
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx),
            get_display_name=_display_close_plan,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="submit_to_queue",
            name="Dispatch to Queue",
            description="dispatch",
            category=CommandCategory.ACTION,
            shortcut="s",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.plan_url is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_submit_to_queue,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="land_pr",
            name="Land PR",
            description="land",
            category=CommandCategory.ACTION,
            shortcut=None,
            is_available=lambda ctx: (
                _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.pr_number is not None and ctx.row.pr_state == "OPEN"
            ),
            get_display_name=_display_land_pr,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="fix_conflicts_remote",
            name="Fix Conflicts Remote",
            description="fix-conflicts",
            category=CommandCategory.ACTION,
            shortcut="5",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.pr_number is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_fix_conflicts_remote,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="address_remote",
            name="Address Remote",
            description="address",
            category=CommandCategory.ACTION,
            shortcut=None,
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.pr_number is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_address_remote,
        ),
        # === OBJECTIVE ACTIONS ===
        CommandDefinition(
            id="one_shot_plan",
            name="Plan (One-Shot)",
            description="plan (one-shot)",
            category=CommandCategory.ACTION,
            shortcut="s",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_objectives_view(ctx),
            get_display_name=_display_one_shot_plan,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="check_objective",
            name="Check Objective",
            description="check",
            category=CommandCategory.ACTION,
            shortcut="5",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_objectives_view(ctx),
            get_display_name=_display_check_objective,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="close_objective",
            name="Close Objective",
            description="close",
            category=CommandCategory.ACTION,
            shortcut=None,
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_objectives_view(ctx),
            get_display_name=_display_close_objective,
        ),
        # === PLAN OPENS ===
        CommandDefinition(
            id="open_issue",
            name="Issue",
            description="plan",
            category=CommandCategory.OPEN,
            shortcut="i",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.plan_url is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_open_issue,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="open_pr",
            name="PR",
            description="pr",
            category=CommandCategory.OPEN,
            shortcut="p",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.pr_url is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_open_pr,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="open_run",
            name="Workflow Run",
            description="run",
            category=CommandCategory.OPEN,
            shortcut="r",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.run_url is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_open_run,
        ),
        # === OBJECTIVE OPENS ===
        CommandDefinition(
            id="open_objective",
            name="Objective",
            description="objective",
            category=CommandCategory.OPEN,
            shortcut="i",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_objectives_view(ctx) and ctx.row.plan_url is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_open_objective,
        ),
        # === PLAN COPIES ===
        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_checkout",
            name="erk br co <branch>",
            description="checkout",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut="c",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.worktree_branch is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_copy_checkout,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_pr_checkout",
            name="checkout && sync",
            description="sync",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut="e",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.pr_number is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_copy_pr_checkout,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_implement_local",
            name="checkout && implement",
            description="implement",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut="2",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.pr_number is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_copy_implement_local,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_submit",
            name="erk pr dispatch",
            description="submit",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut="3",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx),
            get_display_name=_display_copy_submit,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_replan",
            name="erk pr replan",
            description="replan",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut="6",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.plan_url is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_copy_replan,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_land",
            name="erk land",
            description="land",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut=None,
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.pr_number is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_copy_land,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_close_plan",
            name="erk pr close",
            description="close",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut=None,
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx),
            get_display_name=_display_copy_close_plan,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_fix_conflicts_remote",
            name="erk launch pr-fix-conflicts",
            description="fix-conflicts",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut=None,
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.pr_number is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_copy_fix_conflicts_remote,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_address_remote",
            name="erk launch pr-address",
            description="address",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut=None,
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.pr_number is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_copy_address_remote,
        ),
        # === OBJECTIVE COPIES ===
        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_plan",
            name="erk objective plan",
            description="plan",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut="1",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_objectives_view(ctx),
            get_display_name=_display_copy_plan,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_view",
            name="erk objective view",
            description="view",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut="3",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_objectives_view(ctx),
            get_display_name=_display_copy_view,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="codespace_run_plan",
            name="Codespace Run Plan",
            description="codespace",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut=None,
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_objectives_view(ctx),
            get_display_name=_display_codespace_run_plan,
        ),
    ]


def get_available_commands(ctx: CommandContext) -> list[CommandDefinition]:
    """Return commands available in current context.

    Args:
        ctx: Command context containing the plan row data

    Returns:
        List of commands that are available for the given context
    """
    return [cmd for cmd in get_all_commands() if cmd.is_available(ctx)]


def get_display_name(cmd: CommandDefinition, ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Get the display name for a command in the given context.

    Args:
        cmd: The command definition
        ctx: The command context

    Returns:
        The dynamic display name if get_display_name is set, otherwise the static name
    """
    if cmd.get_display_name is not None:
        return cmd.get_display_name(ctx)
    return cmd.name

This module defines all available commands and their availability predicates.
Commands are organized by category: Actions, Opens, Copies.
Plan commands appear only in Plans/Learn views; objective commands appear only in Objectives view.
"""

from erk.tui.commands.types import CommandCategory, CommandContext, CommandDefinition
from erk.tui.views.types import ViewMode

CATEGORY_EMOJI: dict[CommandCategory, str] = {
    CommandCategory.ACTION: "⚡",
    CommandCategory.OPEN: "🔗",
    CommandCategory.COPY: "📋",
}


# === View Mode Predicates ===


def _is_plan_view(ctx: CommandContext) -> bool:

def get_available_commands(ctx: CommandContext) -> list[CommandDefinition]:
    """Return commands available in current context.

    Args:
        ctx: Command context containing the plan row data

    Returns:
        List of commands that are available for the given context
    """
    return [cmd for cmd in get_all_commands() if cmd.is_available(ctx)]


def get_display_name(cmd: CommandDefinition, ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Get the display name for a command in the given context.

    Args:
        cmd: The command definition
        ctx: The command context

    Returns:
        The dynamic display name if get_display_name is set, otherwise the static name
    """
    if cmd.get_display_name is not None:
        return cmd.get_display_name(ctx)
    return cmd.name

Plan commands appear only in Plans/Learn views; objective commands appear only in Objectives view.

    CommandCategory.COPY: "📋",
}

def get_available_commands(ctx: CommandContext) -> list[CommandDefinition]:
    """Return commands available in current context.

    Args:
        ctx: Command context containing the plan row data

    Returns:
        List of commands that are available for the given context
    """
    return [cmd for cmd in get_all_commands() if cmd.is_available(ctx)]


def get_display_name(cmd: CommandDefinition, ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Get the display name for a command in the given context.

    Args:
        cmd: The command definition
        ctx: The command context

    Returns:
        The dynamic display name if get_display_name is set, otherwise the static name
    """
    if cmd.get_display_name is not None:
        return cmd.get_display_name(ctx)
    return cmd.name

"""Command registry for command palette.

This module defines all available commands and their availability predicates.
Commands are organized by category: Actions, Opens, Copies.
Plan commands appear only in Plans/Learn views; objective commands appear only in Objectives view.
"""

from erk.tui.commands.types import CommandCategory, CommandContext, CommandDefinition
from erk.tui.views.types import ViewMode

CATEGORY_EMOJI: dict[CommandCategory, str] = {
    CommandCategory.ACTION: "⚡",
    CommandCategory.OPEN: "🔗",
    CommandCategory.COPY: "📋",
}


# === View Mode Predicates ===


def _is_plan_view(ctx: CommandContext) -> bool:
    """True when not in Objectives view (i.e., Plans or Learn)."""
    return ctx.view_mode != ViewMode.OBJECTIVES


def _is_objectives_view(ctx: CommandContext) -> bool:
    """True when in Objectives view."""
    return ctx.view_mode == ViewMode.OBJECTIVES


# === Display Name Generators (Plan Commands) ===


def _display_close_plan(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for close_plan command."""
    return f"erk pr close {ctx.row.plan_id}"


def _display_dispatch_to_queue(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for dispatch_to_queue command."""
    return f"erk pr dispatch {ctx.row.plan_id}"


def _display_land_pr(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for land_pr command."""
    return f"erk land {ctx.row.pr_number}"


def _display_fix_conflicts_remote(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for fix_conflicts_remote command."""
    return f"erk launch pr-fix-conflicts --pr {ctx.row.pr_number}"


def _display_address_remote(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for address_remote command."""
    return f"erk launch pr-address --pr {ctx.row.pr_number}"


def _display_open_issue(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for open_issue command."""
    if ctx.row.plan_url:
        return ctx.row.plan_url
    return "Issue"


def _display_open_pr(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for open_pr command."""
    if ctx.row.pr_url:
        return ctx.row.pr_url
    return "PR"


def _display_open_run(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for open_run command."""
    if ctx.row.run_url:
        return ctx.row.run_url
    return "Workflow Run"


def _display_copy_checkout(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_checkout command."""
    if ctx.row.worktree_branch:
        return f"erk br co {ctx.row.worktree_branch}"
    if ctx.row.pr_number:
        return f"erk pr co {ctx.row.pr_number}"
    return "erk br co <branch>"


def _display_copy_pr_checkout(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_pr_checkout command."""
    if ctx.row.pr_number:
        pr = ctx.row.pr_number
        return f'source "$(erk pr checkout {pr} --script --sync)"'
    return "checkout"


def _display_copy_implement_local(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_implement_local command."""
    return f'source "$(erk pr checkout {ctx.row.pr_number} --script)" && erk implement --dangerous'


def _display_copy_dispatch(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_dispatch command."""
    return f"erk pr dispatch {ctx.row.plan_id}"


def _display_copy_replan(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_replan command."""
    return f"erk pr replan {ctx.row.plan_id}"


def _display_copy_land(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_land command."""
    return f"erk land {ctx.row.pr_number}"


def _display_copy_close_plan(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_close_plan command."""
    return f"erk pr close {ctx.row.plan_id}"


def _display_copy_fix_conflicts_remote(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_fix_conflicts_remote command."""
    return f"erk launch pr-fix-conflicts --pr {ctx.row.pr_number}"


def _display_copy_address_remote(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_address_remote command."""
    return f"erk launch pr-address --pr {ctx.row.pr_number}"


# === Display Name Generators (Objective Commands) ===


def _display_one_shot_plan(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for one_shot_plan command."""
    return f"erk objective plan {ctx.row.plan_id} --one-shot"


def _display_check_objective(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for check_objective command."""
    return f"erk objective check {ctx.row.plan_id}"


def _display_close_objective(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for close_objective command."""
    return f"erk objective close {ctx.row.plan_id} --force"


def _display_codespace_run_plan(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for codespace_run_plan command."""
    return f"erk codespace run objective plan {ctx.row.plan_id}"


def _display_open_objective(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for open_objective command."""
    if ctx.row.plan_url:
        return ctx.row.plan_url
    return "Objective"


def _display_copy_plan(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_plan command."""
    return f"erk objective plan {ctx.row.plan_id}"


def _display_copy_view(ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Display name for copy_view command."""
    return f"erk objective view {ctx.row.plan_id}"


def get_all_commands() -> list[CommandDefinition]:
    """Return all command definitions.

    Commands are ordered by category:
    1. Actions (mutative operations)
    2. Opens (browser navigation)
    3. Copies (clipboard operations)

    Plan commands are filtered out in Objectives view; objective commands
    are filtered out in Plans/Learn views.

    Returns:
        List of all available command definitions
    """
    return [
        # === PLAN ACTIONS ===
        CommandDefinition(
            id="close_plan",
            name="Close Plan",
            description="close",
            category=CommandCategory.ACTION,
            shortcut=None,
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx),
            get_display_name=_display_close_plan,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="dispatch_to_queue",
            name="Dispatch to Queue",
            description="dispatch",
            category=CommandCategory.ACTION,
            shortcut="s",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.plan_url is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_dispatch_to_queue,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="land_pr",
            name="Land PR",
            description="land",
            category=CommandCategory.ACTION,
            shortcut=None,
            is_available=lambda ctx: (
                _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.pr_number is not None and ctx.row.pr_state == "OPEN"
            ),
            get_display_name=_display_land_pr,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="fix_conflicts_remote",
            name="Fix Conflicts Remote",
            description="fix-conflicts",
            category=CommandCategory.ACTION,
            shortcut="5",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.pr_number is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_fix_conflicts_remote,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="address_remote",
            name="Address Remote",
            description="address",
            category=CommandCategory.ACTION,
            shortcut=None,
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.pr_number is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_address_remote,
        ),
        # === OBJECTIVE ACTIONS ===
        CommandDefinition(
            id="one_shot_plan",
            name="Plan (One-Shot)",
            description="plan (one-shot)",
            category=CommandCategory.ACTION,
            shortcut="s",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_objectives_view(ctx),
            get_display_name=_display_one_shot_plan,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="check_objective",
            name="Check Objective",
            description="check",
            category=CommandCategory.ACTION,
            shortcut="5",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_objectives_view(ctx),
            get_display_name=_display_check_objective,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="close_objective",
            name="Close Objective",
            description="close",
            category=CommandCategory.ACTION,
            shortcut=None,
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_objectives_view(ctx),
            get_display_name=_display_close_objective,
        ),
        # === PLAN OPENS ===
        CommandDefinition(
            id="open_issue",
            name="Issue",
            description="plan",
            category=CommandCategory.OPEN,
            shortcut="i",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.plan_url is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_open_issue,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="open_pr",
            name="PR",
            description="pr",
            category=CommandCategory.OPEN,
            shortcut="p",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.pr_url is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_open_pr,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="open_run",
            name="Workflow Run",
            description="run",
            category=CommandCategory.OPEN,
            shortcut="r",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.run_url is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_open_run,
        ),
        # === OBJECTIVE OPENS ===
        CommandDefinition(
            id="open_objective",
            name="Objective",
            description="objective",
            category=CommandCategory.OPEN,
            shortcut="i",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_objectives_view(ctx) and ctx.row.plan_url is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_open_objective,
        ),
        # === PLAN COPIES ===
        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_checkout",
            name="erk br co <branch>",
            description="checkout",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut="c",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.worktree_branch is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_copy_checkout,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_pr_checkout",
            name="checkout && sync",
            description="sync",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut="e",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.pr_number is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_copy_pr_checkout,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_implement_local",
            name="checkout && implement",
            description="implement",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut="2",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.pr_number is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_copy_implement_local,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_submit",
            name="erk pr dispatch",
            description="submit",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut="3",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx),
            get_display_name=_display_copy_submit,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_replan",
            name="erk pr replan",
            description="replan",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut="6",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.plan_url is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_copy_replan,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_land",
            name="erk land",
            description="land",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut=None,
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.pr_number is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_copy_land,

        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_close_plan",

            shortcut=None,
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx),
            get_display_name=_display_copy_close_plan,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_fix_conflicts_remote",
            name="erk launch pr-fix-conflicts",
            description="fix-conflicts",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut=None,
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.pr_number is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_copy_fix_conflicts_remote,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_address_remote",
            name="erk launch pr-address",
            description="address",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut=None,
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_plan_view(ctx) and ctx.row.pr_number is not None,
            get_display_name=_display_copy_address_remote,
        ),
        # === OBJECTIVE COPIES ===
        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_plan",
            name="erk objective plan",
            description="plan",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut="1",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_objectives_view(ctx),
            get_display_name=_display_copy_plan,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="copy_view",
            name="erk objective view",
            description="view",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut="3",
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_objectives_view(ctx),
            get_display_name=_display_copy_view,
        ),
        CommandDefinition(
            id="codespace_run_plan",
            name="Codespace Run Plan",
            description="codespace",
            category=CommandCategory.COPY,
            shortcut=None,
            is_available=lambda ctx: _is_objectives_view(ctx),
            get_display_name=_display_codespace_run_plan,
        ),
    ]


def get_available_commands(ctx: CommandContext) -> list[CommandDefinition]:
    """Return commands available in current context.

    Args:
        ctx: Command context containing the plan row data

    Returns:
        List of commands that are available for the given context
    """
    return [cmd for cmd in get_all_commands() if cmd.is_available(ctx)]


def get_display_name(cmd: CommandDefinition, ctx: CommandContext) -> str:
    """Get the display name for a command in the given context.

    Args:
        cmd: The command definition
        ctx: The command context

    Returns:
        The dynamic display name if get_display_name is set, otherwise the static name
    """
    if cmd.get_display_name is not None:
        return cmd.get_display_name(ctx)
    return cmd.name

"""Tests for command palette registry."""

from erk.tui.commands.registry import get_all_commands, get_available_commands, get_display_name
from erk.tui.commands.types import CommandCategory, CommandContext
from erk.tui.views.types import ViewMode

def test_all_commands_have_unique_ids() -> None:
    """All commands should have unique IDs."""
    commands = get_all_commands()
    ids = [cmd.id for cmd in commands]
    assert len(ids) == len(set(ids)), "Command IDs must be unique"


def test_all_commands_have_required_fields() -> None:
    """All commands should have required fields populated."""
    commands = get_all_commands()
    for cmd in commands:
        assert cmd.id, f"Command missing id: {cmd}"
        assert cmd.name, f"Command {cmd.id} missing name"
        assert cmd.description, f"Command {cmd.id} missing description"
        assert isinstance(cmd.category, CommandCategory), f"Command {cmd.id} missing valid category"
        assert callable(cmd.is_available), f"Command {cmd.id} missing is_available"

# === Palette Display Formatting Tests ===


def test_format_palette_display_produces_styled_text() -> None:
    """_format_palette_display produces Text with correct structure and dim command."""
    from rich.text import Text

    from erk.tui.commands.provider import _format_palette_display

    result = _format_palette_display("⚡", "close", "erk pr close 123")

    # Result should be a Text object
    assert isinstance(result, Text)

    # Plain text should match expected format
    assert result.plain == "⚡ close: erk pr close 123"

    # Command portion should be dimmed
    # Check that "dim" style is applied to the command text
    spans = list(result.spans)
    # The structure is: emoji + " ", label + ": ", (command_text, "dim")
    # Find the span covering the command text portion
    command_start = len("⚡ close: ")
    command_span = next((s for s in spans if s.start == command_start), None)
    assert command_span is not None, "Expected span for command text"
    assert command_span.style == "dim"


def test_format_search_display_preserves_highlighting() -> None:
    """_format_search_display preserves fuzzy match highlights in dim portion."""
    from rich.text import Text

    from erk.tui.commands.provider import _format_search_display

    # Simulate highlighted text from fuzzy matcher
    # e.g., "close: erk pr close 123" with "close" highlighted
    highlighted = Text("close: erk pr close 123")
    highlighted.stylize("bold", 0, 5)  # First "close" highlighted

    result = _format_search_display("⚡", highlighted, len("close"))

    assert isinstance(result, Text)
    assert result.plain == "⚡ close: erk pr close 123"

# === Shortcut Safety Tests ===


def test_shortcuts_no_conflicts_within_view() -> None:
    """Shortcuts should not conflict within the same view mode.

    Plan commands and objective commands can reuse shortcuts (e.g., "i", "s", "1")
    because they are mutually exclusive (filtered by view mode). But within a single
    view, shortcuts must be unique.
    """
    row = make_plan_row(
        123,
        "Test",
        plan_url="https://github.com/test/repo/issues/123",
        pr_number=456,
        pr_url="https://github.com/test/repo/pull/456",
        pr_state="OPEN",
        worktree_branch="feature-123",
        run_url="https://github.com/test/repo/actions/runs/789",
    )

    for view_mode in (ViewMode.PLANS, ViewMode.OBJECTIVES):
        ctx = CommandContext(row=row, view_mode=view_mode)
        commands = get_available_commands(ctx)
        shortcuts = [cmd.shortcut for cmd in commands if cmd.shortcut is not None]
        assert len(shortcuts) == len(set(shortcuts)), (
            f"Duplicate shortcuts in {view_mode.name} view: {shortcuts}"
        )


# === Plan Backend Tests ===

"""Tests for command palette registry."""

from erk.tui.commands.registry import get_all_commands, get_available_commands, get_display_name
from erk.tui.commands.types import CommandCategory, CommandContext
from erk.tui.views.types import ViewMode
from erk_shared.gateway.plan_data_provider.fake import make_plan_row


def test_all_commands_have_unique_ids() -> None:
    """All commands should have unique IDs."""
    commands = get_all_commands()
    ids = [cmd.id for cmd in commands]
    assert len(ids) == len(set(ids)), "Command IDs must be unique"


def test_all_commands_have_required_fields() -> None:
    """All commands should have required fields populated."""
    commands = get_all_commands()
    for cmd in commands:
        assert cmd.id, f"Command missing id: {cmd}"
        assert cmd.name, f"Command {cmd.id} missing name"
        assert cmd.description, f"Command {cmd.id} missing description"
        assert isinstance(cmd.category, CommandCategory), f"Command {cmd.id} missing valid category"
        assert callable(cmd.is_available), f"Command {cmd.id} missing is_available"

# === Palette Display Formatting Tests ===


def test_format_palette_display_produces_styled_text() -> None:
    """_format_palette_display produces Text with correct structure and dim command."""
    from rich.text import Text

    from erk.tui.commands.provider import _format_palette_display

    result = _format_palette_display("⚡", "close", "erk pr close 123")

    # Result should be a Text object
    assert isinstance(result, Text)

    # Plain text should match expected format
    assert result.plain == "⚡ close: erk pr close 123"

    # Command portion should be dimmed
    # Check that "dim" style is applied to the command text
    spans = list(result.spans)
    # The structure is: emoji + " ", label + ": ", (command_text, "dim")
    # Find the span covering the command text portion
    command_start = len("⚡ close: ")
    command_span = next((s for s in spans if s.start == command_start), None)
    assert command_span is not None, "Expected span for command text"
    assert command_span.style == "dim"


def test_format_search_display_preserves_highlighting() -> None:
    """_format_search_display preserves fuzzy match highlights in dim portion."""
    from rich.text import Text

    from erk.tui.commands.provider import _format_search_display

    # Simulate highlighted text from fuzzy matcher
    # e.g., "close: erk pr close 123" with "close" highlighted
    highlighted = Text("close: erk pr close 123")
    highlighted.stylize("bold", 0, 5)  # First "close" highlighted

    result = _format_search_display("⚡", highlighted, len("close"))

    assert isinstance(result, Text)
    assert result.plain == "⚡ close: erk pr close 123"

# === Shortcut Safety Tests ===


def test_shortcuts_no_conflicts_within_view() -> None:
    """Shortcuts should not conflict within the same view mode.

    Plan commands and objective commands can reuse shortcuts (e.g., "i", "s", "1")
    because they are mutually exclusive (filtered by view mode). But within a single
    view, shortcuts must be unique.
    """
    row = make_plan_row(
        123,
        "Test",
        plan_url="https://github.com/test/repo/issues/123",
        pr_number=456,
        pr_url="https://github.com/test/repo/pull/456",
        pr_state="OPEN",
        worktree_branch="feature-123",
        run_url="https://github.com/test/repo/actions/runs/789",
    )

    for view_mode in (ViewMode.PLANS, ViewMode.OBJECTIVES):
        ctx = CommandContext(row=row, view_mode=view_mode)
        commands = get_available_commands(ctx)
        shortcuts = [cmd.shortcut for cmd in commands if cmd.shortcut is not None]
        assert len(shortcuts) == len(set(shortcuts)), (
            f"Duplicate shortcuts in {view_mode.name} view: {shortcuts}"
        )

# === Palette Display Formatting Tests ===


def test_format_palette_display_produces_styled_text() -> None:
    """_format_palette_display produces Text with correct structure and dim command."""
    from rich.text import Text

    from erk.tui.commands.provider import _format_palette_display

    result = _format_palette_display("⚡", "close", "erk pr close 123")

    # Result should be a Text object
    assert isinstance(result, Text)

    # Plain text should match expected format
    assert result.plain == "⚡ close: erk pr close 123"

    # Command portion should be dimmed
    # Check that "dim" style is applied to the command text
    spans = list(result.spans)
    # The structure is: emoji + " ", label + ": ", (command_text, "dim")
    # Find the span covering the command text portion
    command_start = len("⚡ close: ")
    command_span = next((s for s in spans if s.start == command_start), None)
    assert command_span is not None, "Expected span for command text"
    assert command_span.style == "dim"


def test_format_search_display_preserves_highlighting() -> None:
    """_format_search_display preserves fuzzy match highlights in dim portion."""
    from rich.text import Text

    from erk.tui.commands.provider import _format_search_display

    # Simulate highlighted text from fuzzy matcher
    # e.g., "close: erk pr close 123" with "close" highlighted
    highlighted = Text("close: erk pr close 123")
    highlighted.stylize("bold", 0, 5)  # First "close" highlighted

    result = _format_search_display("⚡", highlighted, len("close"))

    assert isinstance(result, Text)
    assert result.plain == "⚡ close: erk pr close 123"

# === Shortcut Safety Tests ===


def test_shortcuts_no_conflicts_within_view() -> None:
    """Shortcuts should not conflict within the same view mode.

    Plan commands and objective commands can reuse shortcuts (e.g., "i", "s", "1")
    because they are mutually exclusive (filtered by view mode). But within a single
    view, shortcuts must be unique.
    """
    row = make_plan_row(
        123,
        "Test",
        plan_url="https://github.com/test/repo/issues/123",
        pr_number=456,
        pr_url="https://github.com/test/repo/pull/456",
        pr_state="OPEN",
        worktree_branch="feature-123",
        run_url="https://github.com/test/repo/actions/runs/789",
    )

    for view_mode in (ViewMode.PLANS, ViewMode.OBJECTIVES):
        ctx = CommandContext(row=row, view_mode=view_mode)
        commands = get_available_commands(ctx)
        shortcuts = [cmd.shortcut for cmd in commands if cmd.shortcut is not None]
        assert len(shortcuts) == len(set(shortcuts)), (
            f"Duplicate shortcuts in {view_mode.name} view: {shortcuts}"
        )

"""Tests for command palette registry."""

from erk.tui.commands.registry import get_all_commands, get_available_commands, get_display_name
from erk.tui.commands.types import CommandCategory, CommandContext
from erk.tui.views.types import ViewMode
from erk_shared.gateway.plan_data_provider.fake import make_plan_row


def test_all_commands_have_unique_ids() -> None:
    """All commands should have unique IDs."""
    commands = get_all_commands()
    ids = [cmd.id for cmd in commands]
    assert len(ids) == len(set(ids)), "Command IDs must be unique"


def test_all_commands_have_required_fields() -> None:
    """All commands should have required fields populated."""
    commands = get_all_commands()
    for cmd in commands:
        assert cmd.id, f"Command missing id: {cmd}"
        assert cmd.name, f"Command {cmd.id} missing name"
        assert cmd.description, f"Command {cmd.id} missing description"
        assert isinstance(cmd.category, CommandCategory), f"Command {cmd.id} missing valid category"
        assert callable(cmd.is_available), f"Command {cmd.id} missing is_available"

# === Palette Display Formatting Tests ===


def test_format_palette_display_produces_styled_text() -> None:
    """_format_palette_display produces Text with correct structure and dim command."""
    from rich.text import Text

    from erk.tui.commands.provider import _format_palette_display

    result = _format_palette_display("⚡", "close", "erk pr close 123")

    # Result should be a Text object
    assert isinstance(result, Text)

    # Plain text should match expected format
    assert result.plain == "⚡ close: erk pr close 123"

    # Command portion should be dimmed
    # Check that "dim" style is applied to the command text
    spans = list(result.spans)
    # The structure is: emoji + " ", label + ": ", (command_text, "dim")
    # Find the span covering the command text portion
    command_start = len("⚡ close: ")
    command_span = next((s for s in spans if s.start == command_start), None)
    assert command_span is not None, "Expected span for command text"
    assert command_span.style == "dim"


def test_format_search_display_preserves_highlighting() -> None:
    """_format_search_display preserves fuzzy match highlights in dim portion."""
    from rich.text import Text

    from erk.tui.commands.provider import _format_search_display

    # Simulate highlighted text from fuzzy matcher
    # e.g., "close: erk pr close 123" with "close" highlighted
    highlighted = Text("close: erk pr close 123")
    highlighted.stylize("bold", 0, 5)  # First "close" highlighted

    result = _format_search_display("⚡", highlighted, len("close"))

    assert isinstance(result, Text)
    assert result.plain == "⚡ close: erk pr close 123"

# === Shortcut Safety Tests ===


def test_shortcuts_no_conflicts_within_view() -> None:
    """Shortcuts should not conflict within the same view mode.

    Plan commands and objective commands can reuse shortcuts (e.g., "i", "s", "1")
    because they are mutually exclusive (filtered by view mode). But within a single
    view, shortcuts must be unique.
    """
    row = make_plan_row(
        123,
        "Test",
        plan_url="https://github.com/test/repo/issues/123",
        pr_number=456,
        pr_url="https://github.com/test/repo/pull/456",
        pr_state="OPEN",
        worktree_branch="feature-123",
        run_url="https://github.com/test/repo/actions/runs/789",
    )

    for view_mode in (ViewMode.PLANS, ViewMode.OBJECTIVES):
        ctx = CommandContext(row=row, view_mode=view_mode)
        commands = get_available_commands(ctx)
        shortcuts = [cmd.shortcut for cmd in commands if cmd.shortcut is not None]
        assert len(shortcuts) == len(set(shortcuts)), (
            f"Duplicate shortcuts in {view_mode.name} view: {shortcuts}"
        )

"""Tests for command palette registry."""

from erk.tui.commands.types import CommandCategory, CommandContext
from erk.tui.views.types import ViewMode

def test_all_commands_have_unique_ids() -> None:
    """All commands should have unique IDs."""
    commands = get_all_commands()
    ids = [cmd.id for cmd in commands]
    assert len(ids) == len(set(ids)), "Command IDs must be unique"

    """All commands should have required fields populated."""
    commands = get_all_commands()
    for cmd in commands:
        assert cmd.id, f"Command missing id: {cmd}"
        assert cmd.name, f"Command {cmd.id} missing name"
        assert cmd.description, f"Command {cmd.id} missing description"
        assert isinstance(cmd.category, CommandCategory), f"Command {cmd.id} missing valid category"
        assert callable(cmd.is_available), f"Command {cmd.id} missing is_available"

# === Palette Display Formatting Tests ===


def test_format_palette_display_produces_styled_text() -> None:
    """_format_palette_display produces Text with correct structure and dim command."""
    from rich.text import Text

    from erk.tui.commands.provider import _format_palette_display

    result = _format_palette_display("⚡", "close", "erk pr close 123")

    # Result should be a Text object
    assert isinstance(result, Text)

    # Plain text should match expected format
    assert result.plain == "⚡ close: erk pr close 123"

    # Command portion should be dimmed
    # Check that "dim" style is applied to the command text
    spans = list(result.spans)
    # The structure is: emoji + " ", label + ": ", (command_text, "dim")
    # Find the span covering the command text portion
    command_start = len("⚡ close: ")
    command_span = next((s for s in spans if s.start == command_start), None)
    assert command_span is not None, "Expected span for command text"
    assert command_span.style == "dim"


def test_format_search_display_preserves_highlighting() -> None:
    """_format_search_display preserves fuzzy match highlights in dim portion."""
    from rich.text import Text

    from erk.tui.commands.provider import _format_search_display

    # Simulate highlighted text from fuzzy matcher
    # e.g., "close: erk pr close 123" with "close" highlighted
    highlighted = Text("close: erk pr close 123")
    highlighted.stylize("bold", 0, 5)  # First "close" highlighted

    result = _format_search_display("⚡", highlighted, len("close"))

    assert isinstance(result, Text)
    assert result.plain == "⚡ close: erk pr close 123"

    assert get_display_name(cmd, ctx) == "erk objective view 7100"


# === Shortcut Safety Tests ===


def test_shortcuts_no_conflicts_within_view() -> None:
    """Shortcuts should not conflict within the same view mode.

    Plan commands and objective commands can reuse shortcuts (e.g., "i", "s", "1")
    because they are mutually exclusive (filtered by view mode). But within a single
    view, shortcuts must be unique.
    """
    row = make_plan_row(
        123,
        "Test",
        plan_url="https://github.com/test/repo/issues/123",
        pr_number=456,
        pr_url="https://github.com/test/repo/pull/456",
        pr_state="OPEN",
        worktree_branch="feature-123",
        run_url="https://github.com/test/repo/actions/runs/789",
    )

    for view_mode in (ViewMode.PLANS, ViewMode.OBJECTIVES):
        ctx = CommandContext(row=row, view_mode=view_mode)
        commands = get_available_commands(ctx)
        shortcuts = [cmd.shortcut for cmd in commands if cmd.shortcut is not None]
        assert len(shortcuts) == len(set(shortcuts)), (
            f"Duplicate shortcuts in {view_mode.name} view: {shortcuts}"
        )


# === Plan Backend Tests ===


def test_commands_available_in_plans_view() -> None:
    """Plan commands should be available in plans view."""
    row = make_plan_row(
        123,
        "Test",
        plan_url="https://github.com/test/repo/pull/123",
        pr_number=456,
        pr_url="https://github.com/test/repo/pull/456",
        pr_state="OPEN",
        worktree_branch="feature-123",
        run_url="https://github.com/test/repo/actions/runs/789",
    )
    ctx = CommandContext(row=row, view_mode=ViewMode.PLANS)
    commands = get_available_commands(ctx)
    cmd_ids = [cmd.id for cmd in commands]

    # These should all be available
    expected_available = [
        "close_plan",
        "dispatch_to_queue",
        "fix_conflicts_remote",
        "address_remote",
        "land_pr",
        "open_issue",
        "open_pr",
        "open_run",
        "copy_checkout",
        "copy_pr_checkout",
        "copy_dispatch",
        "copy_replan",
        "copy_land",
        "copy_close_plan",
        "copy_fix_conflicts_remote",
        "copy_address_remote",
        "copy_implement_local",
    ]
    for cmd_id in expected_available:
        assert cmd_id in cmd_ids, f"Command {cmd_id} should be available in plans view"
